name: usd_ois_calib_then_price_5y_irs
description: Synthetic USD OIS calibrate-then-price integration fixture with flattened curve and IRS outputs.

provenance.source_detail: Deterministic synthetic rates integration fixture with flattened calibration and pricing outputs
provenance.regen_command: manual formula fixture; flattened outputs committed in fixture

source_validation.reason: Nested integration calibration contains final curve/parameter outputs but lacks executable quote/source instruments; this section is source-validation only while the pricing leg executes.
source_validation.required_for_execution: Add executable market quotes/source instruments and wire this integration fixture to the real calibration engine so calibration metrics are computed from residuals.

source_reference.non_compared_metrics_reason: Nested pricing leg keeps the Bloomberg IRS source NPV, but the executable pricing fixture has a documented corrected compounded-SOFR curve-proxy residual.

source_validation.reason: Integration fixture contains source calibration/pricing outputs, but the nested calibration leg is source-validation and the nested IRS pricing leg is non-gating due to documented Bloomberg-vs-model residuals. Keep this non-gating until both legs execute like-for-like.
source_validation.required_for_execution: Reconcile the canonical pricing/calibration inputs and risk methodology to the external source, then remove this source_validation block so expected_outputs are compared by Rust and Python runners.

irs_npv.tolerance_reason: Matches pricing/irs/usd_sofr_5y_receive_fixed_swpm.json screen-level Bloomberg tolerance. abs-or-rel: absolute/display tolerance is the intended comparison gate; relative tolerance is retained only as a legacy diagnostic.

irs_dv01.tolerance_reason: Matches pricing/irs/usd_sofr_5y_receive_fixed_swpm.json signed receive-fixed DV01 convention tolerance. abs-or-rel: absolute/display tolerance is the intended comparison gate; relative tolerance is retained only as a legacy diagnostic.
